description: 'philo' names either the light part of the daily cycle or one complete light-and-dark cycle, according to context. A count ordinarily selects the complete cycle; contrast with 'shero' (night) selects the hours of daylight.
articulatory_notes: Bring the lips close without sealing them and pass unvoiced air through the narrow opening for 'ph', then spread into stressed high-front 'i'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for 'l' and draw it back while rounding into middle-height 'o'.
usage_notes: Phi forms 'ha philo' (today), 'luera philo' (yesterday), 'wireo philo' (tomorrow), and 'theula philo' (every day). Counted days take a numeral directly, with optional classifier 'nophe'.

examples.translation: The child played during the day.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The visible cycle of light and darkness remains a practical reference for work, rest, travel, and memory.

semantic_domains.nature: the recurring light-and-dark cycle shaped by the sun
semantic_domains.temporal: a daylight span or one complete daily cycle
